verses.text: God bids them go up to the land, but will not go among them, lest He consume them. Withheld presence. Sin threatens the nearness they had.
God bids them go up to the land, but will not go among them, lest He consume them. Withheld presence. Sin threatens the nearness they had.
God bids them go up to the land, but will not go among them, lest He consume them. Withheld presence. Sin threatens the nearness they had.
The people mourn and strip off their ornaments. Mourning. Loss of God's presence is bitter.
The people mourn and strip off their ornaments. Mourning. Loss of God's presence is bitter.
The people mourn and strip off their ornaments. Mourning. Loss of God's presence is bitter.
Moses pitches the tent of meeting outside the camp. The tent. He seeks God apart from the defiled camp.
Moses pitches the tent of meeting outside the camp. The tent. He seeks God apart from the defiled camp.
The LORD speaks to Moses 'face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend.' Friendship. God draws near to His faithful servant.
The LORD speaks to Moses 'face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend.' Friendship. God draws near to His faithful servant.
The LORD speaks to Moses 'face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend.' Friendship. God draws near to His faithful servant.
Moses asks to know God's way, 'that I may find grace in thy sight.' Seeking. He longs to know God Himself.
Moses asks to know God's way, 'that I may find grace in thy sight.' Seeking. He longs to know God Himself.
God promises, 'My presence shall go with thee'; this marks them as His. Presence. God's company is their distinction.
God promises, 'My presence shall go with thee'; this marks them as His. Presence. God's company is their distinction.
God promises, 'My presence shall go with thee'; this marks them as His. Presence. God's company is their distinction.
Moses asks, 'I beseech thee, shew me thy glory.' Bold request. He hungers for more of God.
Moses asks, 'I beseech thee, shew me thy glory.' Bold request. He hungers for more of God.
God will show His goodness, but 'there shall no man see me, and live.' Goodness. God reveals mercy, hiding His full glory.
God will show His goodness, but 'there shall no man see me, and live.' Goodness. God reveals mercy, hiding His full glory.
Moses is hidden in a rock to see God's 'back parts.' Sheltered. God shields him while passing by.
